steps.title: Compare prey types
steps.content: Text 2 explains that many predation-relief studies showing prey increases focus on small, rapidly reproducing prey such as birds, mice, and rabbits. Text 1's ash-grey mice fit that category, while Beaulieu and Dufour's moose are large and slowly reproducing.

steps.title: Reject unsupported comparisons
steps.content: B invents a predator-to-prey body-size ratio. C shifts the issue to predator traits, but Text 2 compares prey traits. D says the response is limited to ash-grey mice, contradicting Text 2's reference to many small prey species. The remaining choice links Grant's findings to mouse traits not shared by moose. The answer is A.
